perturbation.original_text: The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for the timely filing of such amendments and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning him or it contained herein or therein, but shall not be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the others, except to the extent that he or it knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate.
perturbation.changed_text: The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning him or it contained herein or therein, but shall not be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the others.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting 'except to the extent that he or it knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate,' the changed text eliminates the 'reason to believe' clause. This creates an in-text contradiction because previously, if a party had reason to believe other party's information was inaccurate, they had some responsibility. Now, that responsibility is entirely removed, potentially conflicting with general legal principles of due diligence and good faith. This introduces ambiguity about the signatories liabilities if they suspect inaccuracies in the fillings made by other undersigned parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning him or it contained herein or therein, but shall not be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the others.
perturbation.justification: The original text placed a responsibility on signatories if they had reason to believe information provided by other parties was inaccurate, whereas the changed text removes that responsibility entirely. This contradicts the general expectation of due diligence and good faith in contracts. The contradicted text, which is identical to the changed text, reflects this removal of responsibility, creating a direct conflict with the original intent of shared responsibility based on awareness. The change makes the agreement internally inconsistent because it eliminates a condition under which a party might have been liable, creating a scenario where a party can ignore known or suspected inaccuracies from other signatories, removing recourse.

perturbation.original_text: DATED: April 8, 2020 SCULPTOR CAPITAL LP By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Chief Financial Officer SCULPTOR CAPITAL HOLDING CORPORATION By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Chief Financial Officer SCULPTOR MANAGEMENT, INC. By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Chief Financial Officer SCULPTOR MASTER FUND LTD By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Chief Financial Officer
perturbation.changed_text: DATED: April 8, 2020 SCULPTOR CAPITAL LP By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Chief Financial Officer SCULPTOR CAPITAL HOLDING CORPORATION By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Chief Financial Officer SCULPTOR MANAGEMENT, INC. By: /s/ Thomas Sipp Thomas Sipp Chief Financial Officer
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the signature and details for 'SCULPTOR MASTER FUND LTD', the document now implies that only the listed entities are part of the joint filing. This directly contradicts the opening statement of the document which should cover all parties involved in the agreement. This omission creates uncertainty regarding the legal obligations and liabilities of SCULPTOR MASTER FUND LTD and creates an in-text contradiction to what the overall document is initially outlining.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The undersigned acknowledge and agree that the foregoing statement on this Schedule 13G is filed on behalf of each of the undersigned and that all subsequent amendments to this statement on Schedule 13G shall be filed on behalf of each of the undersigned without the necessity of filing additional joint acquisition statements. The undersigned acknowledge that each shall be responsible for the timely filing of such amendments and for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning him or it contained herein or therein, but shall not be responsible for the completeness and accuracy of the information concerning the others, except to the extent that he or it knows or has reason to believe that such information is inaccurate.
perturbation.justification: The omission of 'SCULPTOR MASTER FUND LTD' from the signature block, as described in the 'Perturbation Explanation', directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text'. The 'Contradicted Text' states that the Schedule 13G is filed on behalf of *each* of the undersigned, implying all entities listed in the original signature block are part of the agreement. By removing 'SCULPTOR MASTER FUND LTD', the changed text implies it is not a party to the agreement, directly conflicting with the 'Contradicted Text' and creating a strong in-text contradiction.
